title: Partnership Readiness Scorecard
description: A 15-question diagnostic assessment that measures a dental practice owner's readiness across five critical dimensions of partnership potential. Produces a personalized analysis with priority recommendations and a tailored follow-up sequence.

categories.name: Practice Performance
categories.description: Measures the financial health and operational efficiency of the practice, including revenue trajectory, overhead management, and production metrics.

categories.name: Operational Independence
categories.description: Evaluates how dependent the practice is on the owner for day-to-day operations, including team stability, systems maturity, and delegation capacity.

categories.name: Clinical Growth Trajectory
categories.description: Assesses the practice's clinical development momentum, including CE investment, technology adoption, case complexity expansion, and peer learning engagement.

categories.name: Market Positioning
categories.description: Measures the practice's competitive position in its local market, including reputation, referral networks, digital presence, and community integration.

categories.name: Personal Alignment
categories.description: Evaluates the owner's personal readiness for partnership, including lifestyle goals, risk tolerance, legacy planning, and openness to collaborative growth.

questions.text: What is your practice's average annual revenue growth over the last 3 years?

options.text: Declining or flat (0% or negative)
options.description: Revenue stagnation may indicate market saturation, operational inefficiency, or deferred investment.

options.text: Modest growth (1-3%)
options.description: Keeping pace with inflation but not outpacing it. The practice is stable but not accelerating.

options.text: Moderate growth (4-7%)
options.description: Healthy trajectory that exceeds industry average. Indicates active management and market demand.

options.text: Strong growth (8-12%)
options.description: Above-market performance suggesting strong clinical demand, effective marketing, or expanding services.

options.text: Exceptional growth (13%+)
options.description: Rapid expansion that may benefit from infrastructure support to sustain momentum.

questions.text: What is your practice's current overhead percentage?

options.text: Above 75%
options.description: Overhead at this level significantly limits profitability and may indicate structural inefficiencies.
Above industry benchmarks. There is room for margin improvement through operational optimization.
Within healthy range for a well-managed general practice. Room for improvement but fundamentally sound.
Strong financial management. The practice runs lean without sacrificing quality.

options.text: Below 55%
options.description: Exceptional cost management. The practice is highly profitable with well-controlled expenses.

questions.text: How would you describe your collections-to-production ratio?

options.text: Below 90% -- significant revenue leakage
options.description: Collections this low indicate billing system failures, insurance follow-up gaps, or patient payment issues.

options.text: 90-93% -- room for improvement
options.description: Below benchmark. Targeted billing process improvements could recover meaningful revenue.

options.text: 94-96% -- at industry benchmark
options.description: Healthy collections rate. The billing infrastructure is functional and reasonably optimized.

options.text: 97-98% -- above average
options.description: Strong collections discipline. The team is well-trained on billing procedures and follow-up.

options.text: 99%+ -- best in class
options.description: Exceptional collections performance. Tight billing systems with minimal revenue leakage.

questions.text: How long has your longest-tenured team member been with the practice?

options.text: Less than 2 years
options.description: High turnover signals culture or compensation challenges that need to be addressed before partnership.

options.text: 2-4 years
options.description: Building stability but the team has not yet developed deep institutional knowledge.

options.text: 5-8 years
options.description: Solid team tenure. Key staff understand systems and patient relationships are well-established.

options.text: 9-14 years
options.description: Strong team loyalty. The practice culture retains talent and institutional knowledge runs deep.

options.text: 15+ years
options.description: Exceptional retention. The team IS the practice culture, and patients trust the staff as much as the doctor.

questions.text: If you took a two-week vacation tomorrow, what would happen to the practice?

options.text: It would close -- nothing functions without me
options.description: Complete owner dependence is the highest-risk position for a practice and the strongest case for partnership.

options.text: It would struggle significantly -- major disruptions expected
options.description: The practice can survive but not thrive. Key decisions bottleneck at the owner.

options.text: It would manage with some hiccups -- team can handle routine operations
options.description: Functional independence for day-to-day but strategic decisions require the owner.

options.text: It would run smoothly -- team handles almost everything
options.description: Strong operational independence. The team has been empowered with decision-making authority.

options.text: It would thrive -- systems and team are fully self-sustaining
options.description: The practice operates as a business, not a job. Owner absence is barely noticeable operationally.

questions.text: How would you rate your practice management software and technology infrastructure?

options.text: Paper-based or severely outdated systems
options.description: Technology gaps create inefficiency and limit scalability. Partnership can accelerate modernization.

options.text: Basic PMS with manual workarounds for most functions
options.description: The foundation exists but significant manual processes remain. Room for automation.

options.text: Modern PMS with some integrated tools (imaging, scheduling)
options.description: Functional technology stack. Not cutting-edge but gets the job done without major friction.

options.text: Comprehensive digital workflow with most functions automated
options.description: Strong technology adoption. Digital imaging, online scheduling, automated reminders in place.

options.text: Fully digital practice -- CBCT, digital impressions, CAD/CAM, cloud-based everything
options.description: Technology leader. The practice has invested in best-in-class tools and workflows.

questions.text: How many hours of continuing education did you personally complete in the last 12 months?

options.text: Only the minimum required for licensure
options.description: Minimal CE investment suggests clinical complacency or time/resource constraints.

options.text: Slightly above minimum (20-30 hours)
options.description: Meeting obligations but not actively pursuing clinical growth.

options.text: Moderate investment (30-50 hours)
options.description: Active learning that goes beyond requirements. Demonstrates growth mindset.

options.text: Significant investment (50-80 hours)
options.description: Strong commitment to clinical excellence. Likely pursuing new clinical capabilities.

options.text: Extensive investment (80+ hours including hands-on courses)
options.description: Clinical growth is a priority. Hands-on training signals intention to expand services and capabilities.

questions.text: Have you added any new clinical services or procedures in the last 2 years?

options.text: No -- I focus on what I know
options.description: Clinical stagnation can lead to declining patient interest and reduced referral value.

options.text: I have considered it but have not made changes
options.description: Interest in growth exists but barriers (time, training, capital) have prevented action.

options.text: One minor addition (e.g., new material or technique)
options.description: Incremental progress. The practice is evolving but not transforming.

options.text: One significant new service (e.g., implant placement, orthodontics)
options.description: Meaningful clinical expansion that opens new revenue streams and patient segments.

options.text: Multiple new services -- actively expanding clinical capabilities
options.description: Aggressive clinical growth. The practice is positioning for the next generation of dentistry.

questions.text: Do you actively participate in clinical study groups, peer learning, or mentorship?

options.text: No -- I practice independently
options.description: Isolation limits clinical development and exposes the practice to blind spots.

options.text: Occasionally attend lectures or webinars
options.description: Passive learning. Exposure without engagement or accountability.

options.text: Member of a study club that meets regularly
options.description: Active peer engagement. Regular cadence of learning with accountability.

options.text: Active participant in multiple learning communities
options.description: Strong commitment to collaborative learning. Network effects amplify clinical growth.

options.text: I mentor others and am recognized as a clinical leader in my community
options.description: Clinical leadership. The dentist is a force multiplier who elevates peers.

questions.text: How would you describe your practice's online reputation (Google reviews, social presence)?

options.text: Minimal online presence -- few reviews, no active profiles
options.description: Invisible online. In 2026, lack of digital presence is a competitive liability.

options.text: Some reviews but inconsistent -- mix of positive and negative
options.description: Digital footprint exists but is not managed. Reputation is left to chance.

options.text: Solid Google profile with 50+ reviews averaging 4.0+
options.description: Healthy online reputation. The practice is discoverable and generally well-reviewed.

options.text: Strong presence -- 100+ reviews, 4.5+ average, active social media
options.description: Excellent digital positioning. The practice actively manages its online reputation.

options.text: Dominant local presence -- 200+ reviews, 4.7+ average, content marketing
options.description: Market leader online. The practice's digital presence is a competitive moat.

questions.text: How strong is your referral network with other dental and medical providers?

options.text: No formal referral relationships
options.description: Isolated practice. No cross-referral pathways limit growth and case complexity.

options.text: A few informal referral relationships
options.description: Some connections but not systematic. Referrals happen but are not cultivated.

options.text: Established referral relationships with 5-10 providers
options.description: Functional referral network. The practice receives and sends referrals regularly.

options.text: Strong network with regular reciprocal referrals
options.description: Active referral ecosystem. Relationships are maintained and produce consistent volume.

options.text: Extensive network -- I am a referral hub in my market
options.description: The practice is a market anchor. Being a referral hub creates compounding patient flow.

questions.text: How much of your practice's new patient flow comes from marketing vs. word-of-mouth?

options.text: Almost entirely word-of-mouth -- no marketing investment
options.description: Organic growth only. While healthy, this caps growth potential and makes the practice vulnerable to demographic shifts.

options.text: Mostly word-of-mouth with occasional marketing efforts
options.description: Sporadic marketing. Growth relies primarily on existing patient base and reputation.

options.text: Balanced mix -- about 50/50 marketing and word-of-mouth
options.description: Diversified patient acquisition. Both channels contribute meaningfully to growth.

options.text: Strong marketing engine supplemented by word-of-mouth
options.description: Marketing-driven growth with organic amplification. The practice controls its growth trajectory.

options.text: Sophisticated multi-channel marketing with tracked ROI
options.description: Data-driven marketing. The practice knows its CAC, LTV, and optimizes channels accordingly.

questions.text: How many hours per week do you spend on non-clinical tasks (admin, HR, billing, compliance)?

options.text: 20+ hours -- I am the CEO, HR department, and dentist
options.description: Administrative overload. The owner is burning time and energy on non-clinical work that partnership eliminates.

options.text: 15-20 hours -- significant administrative burden
options.description: Heavy non-clinical load. Partnership would reclaim 15+ hours per week for clinical work or personal time.

options.text: 10-15 hours -- manageable but constant
options.description: Moderate administrative load. Enough to feel the weight but not enough to break.

options.text: 5-10 hours -- I have delegated most admin tasks
options.description: Good delegation. The owner has built systems to handle most non-clinical work.

options.text: Under 5 hours -- my team handles virtually everything
options.description: Exceptional delegation. Administrative burden is minimal. Partnership value is elsewhere (equity, education, community).

questions.text: How would you describe your feelings about the next 5-10 years of practice ownership?

options.text: Burned out -- I am not sure how much longer I can do this alone
options.description: Burnout is the strongest signal that the current model is unsustainable. Partnership provides operational relief.

options.text: Tired of the business side but still love clinical work
options.description: Classic partnership candidate. Loves dentistry, hates running a business. Partnership separates the two.

options.text: Content but curious about what else is possible
options.description: Openness to exploration. Not in pain but aware that there might be a better model.

options.text: Satisfied and growing -- but wondering about liquidity or exit planning
options.description: Strategic planning mindset. Partnership is a financial and succession tool, not an escape.

options.text: Thriving -- I love every aspect of ownership
options.description: Full satisfaction with current model. Partnership would need to offer something beyond operational relief.

questions.text: If partnership could deliver one outcome, which would matter most to you?

options.text: Financial liquidity -- I want to de-risk and diversify my net worth
options.description: Financial motivation. SGA's JV equity model delivers immediate liquidity plus ongoing equity appreciation.

options.text: Operational relief -- I want to stop being the CEO and just be a dentist
options.description: Lifestyle motivation. Partnership eliminates the administrative burden that is the #1 source of dentist burnout.

options.text: Clinical growth -- I want access to mentorship, CE, and advanced training
options.description: Growth motivation. SGA's education platform (Pikos Institute, 4Front, Individual Growth Engine) is the strongest differentiator.

options.text: Succession planning -- I want to protect my patients and team after I transition
options.description: Legacy motivation. Partnership ensures continuity of care and employment long after the owner retires.

options.text: Peer community -- I am tired of practicing in isolation
options.description: Connection motivation. The 4Front Symposium and peer collaboration culture address professional isolation directly.

results.name: Foundation Builder
results.headline: You Are Building the Foundation
results.analysis:
Your scorecard indicates that your practice is in an early or rebuilding phase across several key dimensions. This is not a negative assessment -- it is a realistic one. Many of the strongest SGA partnerships have come from practices at exactly this stage, because the operational relief, technology investment, and clinical development resources that partnership provides have the most transformative impact when applied to a practice with strong fundamentals and room to grow.

Your Practice Performance scores suggest opportunities to strengthen revenue trajectory and cost management. Your Operational Independence results indicate that the practice may be heavily dependent on you personally -- which is both a testament to your dedication and a risk factor for sustainability. Your Clinical Growth and Market Positioning scores point to areas where targeted investment (in CE, technology, marketing, and referral networks) could unlock significant upside.

The most important insight from your scorecard is this: the gap between where you are and where you could be is exactly the gap that partnership is designed to close. The practices that gain the most from SGA's model are not the ones that are already optimized -- they are the ones with strong patient relationships, loyal teams, and an owner who knows the current model is not sustainable.

Your next step should be an honest conversation about what is holding the practice back -- and whether those barriers are ones you can solve alone or ones that require infrastructure, capital, and community.
recommendations: Schedule a confidential discovery call to explore whether SGA's operational infrastructure could address your specific bottlenecks
Request a copy of the SGA transition timeline to understand what the first 90 days of partnership looks like operationally
Speak with a current SGA partner whose practice was at a similar stage when they joined -- we will connect you directly
Review your overhead percentage and collections ratio with an eye toward specific improvements that partnership support could accelerate
Consider whether access to the Pikos Institute and 4Front Symposium would change your clinical growth trajectory over the next 3 years

results.name: Growth Ready
results.headline: You Are Ready to Grow -- the Question Is How
results.analysis:
Your scorecard reveals a practice that has built solid fundamentals and is now facing the classic inflection point: you have proven you can build a successful practice, and now the question is whether the next phase of growth requires the same model or a different one.

Your Practice Performance scores show a practice that is financially healthy but may be approaching the ceiling of what solo ownership can deliver. Your Operational Independence results suggest that you have built some systems and team capabilities, but the practice still depends on you for key decisions and functions. Your Clinical Growth scores indicate active interest in development but potential barriers (time, capital, access to advanced training) that limit how fast you can expand your capabilities.

The defining characteristic of a Growth Ready practice is that the owner has the ambition and the clinical talent to reach the next level -- but the business model is the constraint. You are spending too many hours on non-clinical work. You want to invest in technology or training but the capital allocation competes with overhead. You know you should be marketing more aggressively but you do not have the bandwidth to manage campaigns.

Partnership at this stage is not about rescue. It is about acceleration. The practices that join SGA from the Growth Ready tier consistently report the fastest clinical development and revenue growth post-partnership, because the operational relief unlocks time and energy that was previously consumed by administrative burden.

Your priority recommendations focus on removing the specific constraints that your scorecard identified as the biggest barriers to your next phase of growth.
recommendations: Identify your single biggest time drain outside of clinical work -- this is the first thing SGA's operational team would eliminate
Request a side-by-side comparison of your current overhead structure versus the SGA-supported model to see where margin improvements are possible
Explore the Individual Growth Engine program -- a 1-on-1 clinical mentorship that has driven 16.5% revenue growth for partners like Dr. Cavendish
Have a conversation with your financial advisor about the JV equity model and what a liquidity event plus retained equity would mean for your net worth diversification
Talk to a current SGA partner in a similar-sized practice about what their first year of partnership looked like operationally and clinically

results.name: Partnership Primed
results.headline: You Have Built Something Exceptional -- Now Amplify It
results.analysis:
Your scorecard tells a clear story: you have built a high-performing practice with strong fundamentals across most dimensions. Your Practice Performance is above average, your team is stable and capable, and your clinical trajectory shows active investment in growth. This is not a practice that needs saving. This is a practice that is ready for multiplication.

The distinction between Growth Ready and Partnership Primed is meaningful. At this tier, the question is not whether partnership could help -- it is whether you are ready to leverage what you have built into something significantly larger. Your practice has the revenue, the team, the clinical quality, and the market positioning that make it a high-value partnership candidate. SGA's model is specifically designed for practices at this level: the operational infrastructure amplifies what is already working, the education platform accelerates clinical growth that is already underway, and the equity model provides liquidity and wealth-building that solo ownership cannot match.

Your scorecard likely shows one or two categories where you scored lower than others. These are your growth edges -- the specific areas where partnership resources would have the most immediate impact. Pay attention to those categories, because they represent the difference between a good practice and an exceptional one.

Practices at the Partnership Primed tier are also the ones most likely to experience what Dr. Cavendish described: 'It is fun again.' When the administrative burden lifts and the clinical community engages, dentists at this level often rediscover why they went into dentistry in the first place.
recommendations: Request a detailed valuation range discussion -- practices at your level typically command premium multiples, and understanding the financial opportunity is the first step
Explore SGA's three-vertical architecture to understand which vertical (Community, Collaborative, or MODIS) aligns with your practice profile and clinical interests
Schedule a call with Dr. Mitch Ellingson (CCO) or Lamonte Jensen (CEO) directly -- practices at your tier warrant a principal-level conversation
Review the Pikos Institute and 4Front Symposium programming to identify specific clinical capabilities you would pursue with partnership support
Discuss the equity model with your financial advisor -- the second bite of the apple (platform equity appreciation) is the mechanism that makes SGA's model financially superior to an outright sale

results.name: Strategic Acquirer
results.headline: You Are Operating at the Highest Level -- Let Us Show You What Is Next
results.analysis:
Your scorecard positions you in the top tier of practice owners we assess. Across Practice Performance, Operational Independence, Clinical Growth, Market Positioning, and Personal Alignment, your practice demonstrates excellence that is rare in the industry. You have built something that most practice owners aspire to but few achieve.

At this tier, partnership is not about fixing problems. It is about strategic leverage. You have the practice quality, the financial performance, and the clinical reputation that command the highest valuations in the market. The question is whether you want to capture that value through a traditional sale (which gives you cash but ends your upside) or through an equity partnership (which gives you cash AND a seat at the table of a $350M+ platform).

SGA's model was designed with practices like yours in mind. The Thurston Group -- 38 years, 8 platforms, $4B+ returned to partners -- provides the patient capital and platform-building expertise that transforms a high-performing practice into a cornerstone of a national network. Your clinical excellence, combined with SGA's education platform, operational infrastructure, and growth capital, creates compounding value that solo ownership cannot replicate.

Practices at the Strategic Acquirer tier often bring additional value to SGA beyond their financial metrics: clinical leadership, mentorship capacity, community reputation, and the ability to attract other high-performing practices to the network. This is partnership in the truest sense -- your contribution to the platform is as valuable as the platform's contribution to you.

Your recommendations at this tier focus on strategic positioning: understanding the full financial opportunity, connecting with SGA's senior leadership, and evaluating whether the long-term equity play aligns with your wealth-building and legacy goals.
recommendations: Schedule a principal-level meeting with Lamonte Jensen and Dr. Mitch Ellingson to discuss strategic partnership at the highest level
Request a detailed pro-forma showing base case, bull case, and bear case equity scenarios -- the second bite math at your practice's valuation level is significant
Explore leadership roles within the SGA network -- clinical advisory board, regional clinical director, or mentorship positions that leverage your expertise across the platform
Connect with the Thurston Group principals (Patrick Haynes III, Daniel Davis) directly to discuss hold period, equity provisions, and historical partner returns

Talk to partners from prior Thurston platforms (Smile Doctors, US Endodontics Partners) who have been through a full investment cycle including exit -- they can describe the equity appreciation firsthand

followUpEmails.subject: Your Partnership Readiness Score: Foundation Builder
subjectVariants: Your Scorecard Results Are In -- Here Is Where You Stand
Dr. [Name], Your Partnership Readiness Assessment
followUpEmails.previewText: Your personalized analysis and the 3 areas with the biggest upside potential.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Thank you for completing the SGA Partnership Readiness Scorecard. Your results are in, and I want to walk you through what they mean -- honestly, specifically, and without any spin.

Your Total Score: [Score] out of 75
Your Tier: Foundation Builder

Here is what this means:

Your practice has solid fundamentals and a foundation worth building on. The scorecard identified specific areas where targeted investment -- in operations, technology, clinical development, or market positioning -- could unlock significant growth.

The most important insight is this: the gap between where you are and where you could be is exactly the gap that partnership is designed to close.

Your Top 3 Priority Areas:

1. [Weakest Category]: This scored lowest in your assessment. It represents the single biggest constraint on your practice's growth trajectory.

2. [Second Weakest Category]: Improvement here would compound with gains in your weakest area.

3. [Third Area]: Targeted attention here rounds out the foundation.

What I would suggest as a next step:

A 15-minute confidential conversation about what is specifically holding your practice back -- and whether those barriers are ones you can solve alone or ones that require infrastructure, capital, and community.

No pitch. No pressure. Just an honest assessment of whether SGA's model addresses your specific situation.

Book a call here: [LINK]

Or simply reply to this email with a good time to connect.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

followUpEmails.subject: The One Area That Could Transform Your Practice
subjectVariants: Your Weakest Category -- and What to Do About It
Dr. [Name], a Deeper Look at Your Scorecard
followUpEmails.previewText: Your scorecard revealed one category that is holding back everything else. Here is what we have seen work.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Three days ago you completed the Partnership Readiness Scorecard. Today I want to dig deeper into your weakest category: [Category Name].

Why this matters more than the other four:

In our experience working with 100+ practice owners, the weakest scorecard category is almost always the root constraint that limits everything else. A practice with strong clinical skills but weak operational independence hits a ceiling because the owner cannot step away to learn new procedures. A practice with great market positioning but poor financial performance cannot invest in the growth that its reputation demands.

[Category Name] is your constraint. And here is the good news: it is also the area where partnership resources have the most transformative impact.

What SGA partners in your situation have experienced:

- Dr. Matt Cavendish scored similarly in clinical growth before partnering. After accessing the Individual Growth Engine mentorship program, he learned all-on-four procedures, grew revenue 16.5%, and described the change as: "It is fun again."

- Partners who scored low in operational independence consistently report that the first thing they notice post-partnership is the time. The hours spent on payroll, HR, compliance, and billing simply disappear. Spencer Pierce, our VP of People and Culture, describes it as: "After onboarding is completed, there is an abundance of relief."

One resource that might help right now:

I would like to send you a case study of a partner whose practice profile was similar to yours at the time they joined. Their story -- the before, the turning point, and the after -- might give you a framework for evaluating your own situation.

Would that be useful? Just reply "yes" and I will send it over.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners

followUpEmails.subject: What Dr. Godat Said About Partnership -- In His Own Words
subjectVariants: A Partner's Perspective: 'Our Partnership Allows Us to Focus on Changing Lives'
What Year 2 Looks Like Inside SGA
followUpEmails.previewText: Real words from a real partner. No script. No filter.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

I have sent you two emails about your scorecard results. Today I want to step back from your numbers and let a current partner speak for himself.

Dr. Mitchel S. Godat runs Periodontal Associates of Memphis. He has been an SGA partner for years. His practice kept its name. His team stayed. His patients noticed nothing except that the office ran smoother.

His words: "SGA supports us and manages things behind the scenes. Our partnership allows us to focus on changing lives."

That is 18 words. And they capture exactly what partnership looks like when it works.

Here is what I want you to consider:

Your scorecard placed you in the Foundation Builder tier. That means there is meaningful upside potential in your practice -- upside that partnership is specifically designed to unlock.

But the scorecard is just numbers. The real question is whether the experience Dr. Godat describes -- operational support, clinical freedom, the ability to focus on patients instead of paperwork -- is something that resonates with where you are right now.

If it does, I would like to connect you directly with a current SGA partner for a peer-to-peer conversation. Not a sales call. A dentist talking to a dentist about what the experience is actually like.

Would a 20-minute peer conversation be valuable? Book one here: [LINK]

Or reply to this email and I will make the introduction personally.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

followUpEmails.subject: Your Partnership Readiness Score: Growth Ready
subjectVariants: Your Scorecard Results -- You Are at an Inflection Point

What this means:

Your Strongest Category: [Strongest Category]

Your Growth Edge: [Weakest Category]

Your Priority Recommendations:

Next Step:

A 20-minute growth strategy session to explore whether your specific constraints are ones that SGA's model addresses. Confidential. No commitment.

Book here: [LINK]

Best,

SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

followUpEmails.subject: The Constraint That Is Costing You the Most
subjectVariants: Your Biggest Growth Barrier -- and How to Remove It
What Your Weakest Scorecard Category Actually Means
followUpEmails.previewText: Your scorecard identified a specific constraint. Here is what practices at your level do about it.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Your scorecard revealed something important: your weakest category is [Category Name], and it is likely the single biggest constraint on your practice's next phase of growth.

Here is why this matters at the Growth Ready tier:

You have the talent, the patient base, and the ambition to reach the next level. But one area is acting as a ceiling -- and the harder you push against it alone, the more time and energy it consumes without producing proportional results.

What we have seen at SGA:

Growth Ready practices that join the network consistently report that the constraint their scorecard identified was the first thing that improved post-partnership. Not because it was easy -- but because the resources to address it were immediately available.

If your constraint is Operational Independence: SGA's centralized operations team absorbs billing, payroll, HR, compliance, and procurement within 60-90 days. Partners describe the experience as going from wearing 15 hats to wearing two.

If your constraint is Clinical Growth: The Pikos Institute, 4Front Symposium, and Individual Growth Engine provide a clinical development pathway that no solo practitioner can access independently. Dr. Cavendish went from clinical complacency to learning all-on-four and growing revenue 16.5% within his first year.

If your constraint is Market Positioning: SGA's marketing infrastructure -- including dedicated support, technology investment, and cross-referral networks -- provides the patient acquisition engine that most practices cannot build or afford independently.

One question to consider:

If your weakest category improved by 2-3 points over the next 12 months, what would that mean for your practice's revenue, your clinical satisfaction, and your quality of life?

That is the math of partnership. And it is specific to you.

Reply to this email with your constraint, and I will send you a case study from a partner who started in the same position.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners

followUpEmails.subject: 'It Is Fun Again' -- What Year 2 Looks Like
subjectVariants: The Story That Changes How You Think About Partnership
Dr. Cavendish Was Where You Are. Here Is What Happened Next.
followUpEmails.previewText: 20 years in practice. Revenues up 16.5%. Staff retention at 100%. His word: fun.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Dr. Matt Cavendish has been in dentistry for 20 years. Good practice. Good numbers. By most external measures, everything was fine.

But in his own words: "When you have been in dentistry for 20 years like I have, sometimes it is not as fun as it used to be."

He was not failing. He was stagnating. The clinical work that used to light him up had become routine. The administrative burden had become suffocating. The isolation of solo ownership had become the water he was swimming in -- invisible but constant.

After partnering with SGA, here is what happened:

- Paired with a clinical mentor through the Individual Growth Engine
- Observed his first all-on-four procedure
- Learned new lab relationships and digital workflows
- Revenue up 16.5% trailing twelve months
- Staff retention at 100%
- EBITDA beating plan by 2.3 points

But here is the number Matt talks about most:

"To be able to do a complex case like this, with somebody who is also working at a very high skill level -- it is fun."

Fun. After 20 years. That word.

Your scorecard placed you at Growth Ready. You are at the exact inflection point where Dr. Cavendish was when he made his decision. The practice is good. The question is whether good is enough -- or whether you want to find out what exceptional feels like.

I would like to connect you with a current SGA partner for a peer conversation. Not a pitch. A dentist talking to a dentist.

Book a 20-minute peer conversation: [LINK]

Or reply and I will make the introduction.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

subjectVariants: Your Scorecard Results -- You Have Built Something Exceptional
Dr. [Name], Your Practice Scored in the Top Tier
followUpEmails.previewText: Your practice is operating at a level that positions you for the highest-value partnerships in the market.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Your Partnership Readiness Scorecard results are in.

Your Total Score: [Score] out of 75
Your Tier: Partnership Primed

Let me be direct: this score places you in the top tier of practice owners we assess. Your practice demonstrates the financial performance, team stability, clinical momentum, and market positioning that we look for in our strongest partnerships.

What your scorecard reveals:

Why Partnership Primed matters financially:

At your practice's performance level, the JV equity model creates the most compelling financial outcome in the market. Here is the math:

- Immediate liquidity: 60-70% of your valuation in cash at closing

followUpEmails.subject: The Financial Model Your Advisor Should See
subjectVariants: Three Scenarios: Base, Bull, Bear -- Which One Is Yours?
The Second Bite of the Apple, Explained
followUpEmails.previewText: Most DSOs show you the bull case. We show you all three. Here is why that matters.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Your scorecard placed you in the Partnership Primed tier. Today I want to go deeper on the financial model, because at your practice's level, the economics of partnership are particularly compelling.

The concept you need to understand: the Second Bite.

In a traditional sale, you receive cash and your financial relationship with the practice ends. In SGA's JV equity model, you receive two financial events:

First bite: Cash at closing (60-70% of your valuation). Immediate liquidity. De-risk your net worth.

Second bite: When the SGA platform has its next liquidity event, your retained equity participates. Based on the Thurston Group's historical returns across 8 platforms ($4B+ returned), this second bite can exceed the first.

The three-scenario analysis:

We show every prospective partner three scenarios: base case, bull case, and bear case. We do this because any organization that only shows you the bull case is hoping you will fall in love before you see the math.

I want you to see all three. And I want your financial advisor to see all three.

Here is what I would suggest:

Let us schedule a 30-minute financial model walkthrough. I will bring the pro-forma, customized to your practice's approximate financials. You bring your advisor. We will walk through the math -- transparently, with every assumption visible.

The worst outcome is your advisor says the numbers do not work. The best outcome is you discover a financial pathway you did not know existed.

Schedule the walkthrough: [LINK]

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners

followUpEmails.subject: Three Questions to Ask Any DSO -- Including Us
subjectVariants: The Framework That Separates Real Partnership From Marketing
If They Cannot Answer These Three Questions, You Have Your Answer
followUpEmails.previewText: These three questions have helped 100+ practice owners make better decisions. Use them on everyone.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

You scored Partnership Primed on the SGA Scorecard. That means you are likely going to have conversations with multiple organizations. Good. You should.

Here are three questions to ask every single one of them -- including us. These come from our experience sitting across the table from 100+ practice owners.

Question 1: What happens to my clinical skills after I sign?

If their answer is "we offer a CE stipend," that is a benefit. It is not a platform. SGA has the Pikos Institute (world-renowned implantology training), the 4Front Symposium (annual peer collaboration), and the Individual Growth Engine (1-on-1 mentorship). That is not a stipend. That is a career inflection point.

Question 2: Who is behind the money, and what is their track record?

The Thurston Group: 38 years. 8 dental and healthcare platforms. $4B+ returned to partners. Founded in 1988. You can verify every number. You can call partners from prior platforms. Ask the other DSOs the same question. If their PE sponsor is undisclosed, that IS the answer.

Question 3: What does your life look like 3 years after signing?

Every DSO sells the transaction. Nobody tells you what year 3 looks like. We can. Dr. Cavendish: revenues up 16.5%, staff retention 100%, clinical skills expanded, "it is fun again." Dr. Godat: "Our partnership allows us to focus on changing lives."

These are not testimonials. These are phone numbers we will give you.

Use these questions on everyone. The organizations that welcome them are the ones worth your time.

When you are ready to apply them to us, we are here: [LINK]

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

followUpEmails.subject: Your Partnership Readiness Score: Strategic Acquirer
subjectVariants: Dr. [Name], You Scored in the Highest Tier
Your Practice Is Operating at the Highest Level We Assess
followUpEmails.previewText: Your scorecard positions you for the most strategic partnership conversations in the market.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Your Partnership Readiness Scorecard results place you at the highest tier we assess.

Your Total Score: [Score] out of 75
Your Tier: Strategic Acquirer

I am not going to give you a boilerplate response. At your level, you deserve direct engagement.

What your score means:

Your practice demonstrates excellence across Practice Performance, Operational Independence, Clinical Growth, Market Positioning, and Personal Alignment. You have built something that most practice owners aspire to but few achieve.

At this tier, partnership is not about fixing problems or filling gaps. It is about strategic leverage. The question is not whether your practice is ready -- it clearly is. The question is whether you want to capture the full value of what you have built through a traditional sale (cash, upside ends) or through a platform equity partnership (cash plus a seat at the table of a $350M+ national network).

What I want to offer you:

A principal-level conversation. Not with a business development representative. With Dr. Mitch Ellingson (CCO and co-founder) or Lamonte Jensen (CEO and co-founder) directly.

Practices at your tier are the cornerstone partnerships that define the network. We invest corresponding time and attention.

I also want to offer direct access to the Thurston Group principals -- Patrick Haynes III and Daniel Davis -- to discuss hold period, equity provisions, and historical partner returns at the level of detail your financial advisors will require.

Schedule a principal-level conversation: [LINK]

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

followUpEmails.subject: The Equity Play at Your Practice's Level
subjectVariants: What $4 Billion Returned to Partners Actually Means for You
The Second Bite Math at Premium Valuations
followUpEmails.previewText: At your valuation level, the JV equity model creates a financial outcome that outright sales cannot match.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

At the Strategic Acquirer tier, the financial conversation is different. Your practice likely commands premium EBITDA multiples, and the structure of the deal matters more than the headline number.

Let me be specific about why the JV equity model creates superior long-term value at your level:

The Traditional Sale:
You receive a premium multiple in cash. The financial relationship ends. You become an employee or consultant. Your upside is capped at the closing check.

The SGA Equity Partnership:
60-70% in cash at closing (immediate liquidity and net worth diversification). 30-40% retained as equity in the SGA platform. As the platform grows -- through additional partnerships, organic growth, and operational improvements -- your equity appreciates.

The Thurston Group has returned $4B+ to partners across 8 dental and healthcare platforms. That historical return is the evidence that the second bite works.

At your practice's valuation level, here is what this means in practice:

The cash at closing provides the liquidity event you need to diversify, fund retirement, or invest elsewhere. The retained equity provides ongoing participation in a platform that is growing. The combination -- when executed by a PE sponsor with a 38-year track record -- has historically delivered total returns that significantly exceed the all-cash alternative.

I want your financial advisor to see this math. Not the pitch deck version -- the term sheet version, with all three scenarios (base, bull, bear) fully modeled.

Let us schedule a financial model session with your advisor present: [LINK]

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners

followUpEmails.subject: The Partners Who Have Been Through a Full Cycle
subjectVariants: Talk to Someone Who Has Been Through a Thurston Exit
Verification, Not Trust -- Here Are the Phone Numbers
followUpEmails.previewText: We do not ask you to trust a claim. We ask you to verify it. Here is how.
followUpEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

At the Strategic Acquirer tier, trust is not built through webinars or emails. It is built through verification.

Here is what I want to offer you -- something no competitor in the market can match:

Direct access to partners from PRIOR Thurston Group platforms who have been through a full investment cycle, including an exit.

Smile Doctors. US Endodontics Partners. US Oral Surgery Management. ARC Health.

These are not current SGA partners with an incentive to recruit. These are dentists and specialists who partnered with Thurston-backed platforms in previous cycles, experienced the partnership, went through the exit, received their equity returns, and can tell you -- with no filter and no agenda -- exactly what the experience was like.

They can answer the questions your financial advisor will ask:
- What were the actual equity returns versus what was projected?
- How long was the hold period?
- What happened to clinical autonomy over the life of the partnership?
- Would you do it again?

No DSO in the market can offer this because no DSO has a PE sponsor with 38 years and 8 platforms of verifiable history.

I do not ask you to trust us. I ask you to verify us. And these conversations are the verification.

Reply to this email and I will provide direct contact information for partners from prior Thurston platforms.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners
[Phone Number]

channels.name: LinkedIn Paid
channels.strategy: Sponsored content and Lead Gen Forms targeting practice owners and specialists. A/B test ad copy variations: pain-point lead (administrative burden), curiosity lead (7 out of 10 statistic), social proof lead (partner results). Geographic targeting: Southeast primary, national secondary. Budget: $3,000-5,000/month.
adCopy: How ready is your practice for the next chapter? Take the 5-minute Partnership Readiness Scorecard and find out where you stand. Confidential. No commitment. Just clarity.
7 out of 10 dentists who sell to a DSO end up frustrated. Are you positioning yourself for the other 30%? Take the Partnership Readiness Scorecard.
Dr. Cavendish scored Growth Ready. 18 months later: revenue up 16.5%, staff retention 100%, and his word for the experience? Fun. Take the scorecard and see where you stand.
channels.targeting: Job titles: Dentist, Practice Owner, DDS, DMD. Company size: 1-50. Industry: Dental, Healthcare. Geography: United States. Age: 35-65.

channels.name: Facebook/Meta Paid
channels.strategy: Awareness and retargeting campaigns. Lookalike audiences built from existing partner profiles. Carousel ads showing before/after partner transformations. Video ads featuring partner testimonials. Budget: $2,000-4,000/month.
adCopy: Still doing payroll at midnight? Still Googling HIPAA compliance? Take the 5-minute Partnership Readiness Scorecard and see if there is a better model for your practice.
QuickBooks no longer calls me to the back office. -- SGA Partner. Could that be you? Find out in 5 minutes.
What would you do with 15 extra hours a week? Take the scorecard. See what is possible.
channels.targeting: Interests: Dental Economics, ADA, Dentistry Today, practice management. Behaviors: Small business owners, healthcare professionals. Age: 35-65.

channels.name: Email Blast
channels.strategy: Send to existing warm leads, webinar registrants, and content downloaders. Segmented by avatar (practice owner vs. specialist vs. associate). Three-email sequence: announcement, reminder, last chance. Personalized subject lines by segment.
adCopy:
Subject: How Ready Is Your Practice? (5-Minute Assessment)
Dr. [Name], we have built a diagnostic scorecard based on our experience with 100+ dental partnerships. It measures 5 dimensions of partnership readiness and gives you a personalized analysis with specific recommendations. Takes 5 minutes. Results are immediate. Confidential.

Subject: Your Scorecard Is Waiting
Dr. [Name], 73 practice owners have taken the Partnership Readiness Scorecard in the last two weeks. The insights are specific -- not generic advice, but recommendations based on YOUR practice's profile.

Subject: Last Day to Take the Scorecard
Dr. [Name], the Partnership Readiness Scorecard closes for this cohort on [Date]. After that, we will compile anonymized benchmark data and the next cohort will not open for 60 days.
channels.targeting: Existing email list segmented by engagement level and avatar type.

channels.name: Organic Social
channels.strategy: LinkedIn posts from Lamonte Jensen and Dr. Mitch Ellingson promoting the scorecard. Content strategy: share anonymized scorecard insights, common patterns from assessments, partner stories that tie back to scorecard categories. 3 posts per week per account.
adCopy: Jensen post: We have assessed 100+ practice owners across 5 dimensions of partnership readiness. The pattern that surprised us most: the highest-scoring practices are not the biggest ones. They are the ones with the strongest teams. Take the scorecard.
Ellingson post: The question I hear most from practice owners: 'How do I know if I am ready?' We built a diagnostic to answer that question honestly. 5 minutes. No pitch. Just clarity.
Ellingson post: Your weakest scorecard category is your growth edge. It is also the area where partnership resources have the most transformative impact. I have seen it 100 times.
channels.targeting: Organic reach through personal networks, engagement pods, and hashtag strategy (#dentistry #practicemanagement #dentalDSO).

channels.name: Website Placement
channels.strategy: Persistent CTA on SGA website homepage, partner stories page, and resources section. Exit-intent popup on high-traffic pages. Embedded scorecard widget on landing pages. SEO-optimized standalone scorecard page targeting 'dental practice valuation' and 'DSO partnership assessment' keywords.
adCopy: Homepage banner: How Ready Is Your Practice? Take the 5-Minute Partnership Readiness Scorecard.
Exit-intent popup: Before you go -- take 5 minutes to see where your practice stands on 5 critical dimensions of partnership readiness.
Resources page CTA: Start Your Assessment -- personalized results in 5 minutes.
channels.targeting: All website visitors, with exit-intent trigger and scroll-depth trigger (50% of page).

channels.name: Conferences
channels.strategy: Promote scorecard at dental conferences via QR codes on booth materials, handout cards, and speaking session slides. Post-conference email to badge scans with scorecard CTA. Target conferences: Hinman, ADA Annual, Greater New York Dental Meeting, Chicago Midwinter, DSO Leadership Summit.
adCopy: Booth card: How Partnership Ready Are You? Scan to take the 5-minute assessment. [QR code]

Post-conference email: Dr. [Name], great meeting you at [Conference]. I mentioned the Partnership Readiness Scorecard during our conversation. Here is the link -- takes 5 minutes and the results are immediate.
Speaking slide: Want to apply the framework we just discussed to YOUR practice? Take the Partnership Readiness Scorecard: [URL]
channels.targeting: Conference attendees who are practice owners or decision-makers.

channels.name: Podcasts
channels.strategy: Promote scorecard during podcast guest appearances. Lamonte Jensen and Dr. Ellingson mention the scorecard as a free resource during interviews on Shared Practices, Investment Grade Practices, Dental Hacks, Dentist Money Show, and other dental podcasts. Custom landing page per podcast for attribution.
adCopy: In-episode mention: If you want to apply the framework we discussed today to your own practice, we have built a free 5-minute scorecard at [URL]. It measures five dimensions of partnership readiness and gives you a personalized analysis. No pitch. Just clarity.
Show notes CTA: Take the Partnership Readiness Scorecard mentioned in this episode: [URL]
channels.targeting: Podcast listeners who are dental practice owners or specialists.

promotionPlan.launchSequence: Week 1: Soft launch to email list (segmented blast). Week 2: Organic social launch (Jensen + Ellingson LinkedIn posts). Week 3: Paid LinkedIn campaign launch. Week 4: Paid Meta campaign launch. Ongoing: Website placement, conference promotion, podcast mentions.
